basicDetails.implementingAgency: Department Of Agriculture & Farmers’ Welfare, Government Of Meghalaya

schemeContent.detailedDescription_md: The “Basic Agriculture Training Centre Scheme” is a State Plan Scheme, which is implemented by the Department of Agriculture &amp; Farmers’ Welfare, Government of Meghalaya. The objective of the scheme is to provide short-term vocational training for rural educated unemployed youths in agriculture and allied sectors to develop entrepreneurship skills and employment generation.
schemeContent.benefits_md:
> **Pattern of Assistance:**


- Free short-term training to educated unemployed rural youths.

> **Type of Assistance and Entitlement (If Any):**


- Training and capacity building.
- Stipend @ ₹500/- per participant per month.
- Seed money @ ₹10,000/- per participant.
schemeContent.briefDescription: The objective of the scheme is to provide short-term vocational training for rural educated unemployed youths in agriculture and allied sectors to develop entrepreneurship skills and employment generation.

children.text: The “Basic Agriculture Training Centre Scheme” is a State Plan Scheme, which is implemented by the Department of Agriculture & Farmers’ Welfare, Government of Meghalaya. The objective of the scheme is to provide short-term vocational training for rural educated unemployed youths in agriculture and allied sectors to develop entrepreneurship skills and employment generation.
Pattern of Assistance:
Free short-term training to educated unemployed rural youths.
Type of Assistance and Entitlement (If Any):
Training and capacity building.
Stipend @ ₹500/- per participant per month.
Seed money @ ₹10,000/- per participant.
Application Process:
Step 01:
The eligible Educated Rural Youths can apply/write an application on plain paper.
Step 02:
Along with the application, attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
Step 03:
Submit the duly filled and signed application along with the relevant documents at the office of the Principal, Basic Agricultural Training Centre (BATC).
Mode of Selection of Beneficiary(s):
The selection procedure is based on merit and on the condition that applicants own agricultural land on which they can start their farm-based activities.
Whom to Contact:
District Agriculture Officers of the concerned districts.
Sub-Divisional Agricultural Officers of the concerned sub-divisions.
District Training Officers, Tura/Shillong.
Agricultural Development Officers of the concerned Community and Rural Development Blocks.

applicationProcess.process_md:
> **Application Process:**

**Step 01:** The eligible Educated Rural Youths can apply/write an application on plain paper.
**Step 02:** Along with the application, attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
**Step 03:** Submit the duly filled and signed application along with the relevant documents at the office of the Principal, Basic Agricultural Training Centre (BATC).
> **Mode of Selection of Beneficiary(s):**

The selection procedure is based on merit and on the condition that applicants own agricultural land on which they can start their farm-based activities. 
> **Whom to Contact:**


   1. District Agriculture Officers of the concerned districts.
   1. Sub-Divisional Agricultural Officers of the concerned sub-divisions.
   1. District Training Officers, Tura/Shillong.
   1. Agricultural Development Officers of the concerned Community and Rural Development Blocks.

eligibilityCriteria.eligibilityDescription_md:
1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of Meghalaya.
1. The applicant should be an unemployed rural youth.
1. The applicant should have passed Class X.
1. The applicant should be interested in starting farm-based activities.
1. The applicant should have owned or leased agricultural land.

children.text: The applicant should be a permanent resident of Meghalaya.
The applicant should be an unemployed rural youth.
The applicant should have passed Class X.
The applicant should be interested in starting farm-based activities.
The applicant should have owned or leased agricultural land.
